title: Krav Noblis

{{Quote|I'm not in this for my health!|Krav Noblis|<ref name="outerrim" />}}
'''Krav Noblis''' was the [[Journalist/Legends|editor]] of ''[[The Adjudicator]]'', a [[Holofeed/Legends|Holofeed]] specifically for [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], during the era of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Noblis often penned columns for ''The Adjudicator'', reporting information gathered from a network of sources and publishing opinions on such subjects as the rise of [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]] in the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunting trade and the effects of the war between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans on the market. Along with running ''The Adjudicator'', Noblis also worked as an active bounty hunter during that period, embarking on hunts that took the writer to locales such as the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]] and the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Until lately, our trade's had a good run. When guys like the Moomo Brothers can get work, anybody can. But if the Mandalorians keep knocking off the customers, those times may be over.|Krav Noblis|<ref name="outerrim" />}}
[[File:Zaynemugshot2-KotORC.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Zayne Carrick (pictured) often featured in Noblis's columns in ''The Adjucator'' in its "Hot List" segment.]]
Krav Noblis was a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who, by [[3963 BBY]], was the [[Journalist/Legends|editor]] of ''[[The Adjudicator]]'', a [[Holofeed/Legends|Holofeed]] specifically for [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunters and "freelance security professionals." 

At one point, Noblis captured a bounty on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]]—however, the world was under a Mandalorian [[Battle of Wayland (Mandalorian Wars)|attack]], and when Noblis brought the target to the [[Unidentified constable|Constable]], the hunter found the [[Constable/Legends|lawman]] leaving with his family. When Noblis asked the Constable for a voucher to take to the nearby [[Lhosan Industries]] building, Noblis was told that the building was full of Mandalorian [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk droids]].<ref name="outerrim" /> 

Noblis frequently wrote columns that were published in ''The Adjudicator'', as well as a feature called "Krav's Hot List," which detailed some of the more notorious and lucrative bounties in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Regulars on the "Hot List" included [[Zayne Carrick]], a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] wanted for allegedly [[Death/Legends|killing]] his Jedi class in the [[Padawan Massacre]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]; [[Marn Hierogryph]], a [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] criminal and Carrick's supposed accomplice; and [[Toki Tollivar|Kelven Garnatrope]], feared as the so-called "[[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] Strangler."<ref name="tools">{{KOTORbackups|20}}</ref>

During Noblis' career at ''The Adjudicator'', Noblis reported on events such as the effects of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mandalorian Wars|invasion]] of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]] on the bounty market, the proliferation of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] entering the bounty hunting trade,<ref name="tools" /> and the strange effect the war was having on corporations that dealt with bounty hunters.<ref name="colonies">{{KOTORbackups|23}}</ref> Noblis cultivated a network of sources and informants that provided the editor with information on companies such as [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Arms]]<ref name="tools" /> and [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania|Adascorp]], inside knowledge that Noblis passed on to the audience at ''The Adjudicator''.<ref name="colonies" />

Through all this, Noblis still found the [[Time/Legends|time]] to be an active bounty hunter during the Mandalorian Wars era. Noblis decided to set out after a "[[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Gorman Vandrayk|Hyro Margryph]],"<ref name="tools" /> who had a bounty on his head for 100,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]],<ref name="outerrim" /> but as soon as Noblis reached the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]], the hunter found that there were multiple autonomous [[HK-24 series assassin droid|HK-24]] [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]] also out for Margryph. Soon afterward, the bounty was canceled.<ref name="tools" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I hate droids. Is that okay? Can I say that without getting hate messages from some 'Be Nice to Your Appliance' association? But I only hate ''specific'' droids—those that take jobs away from hard-working security pros.|Krav Noblis|<ref name="tools" />}}
Despite being an active bounty hunter at the time of the Mandalorian Wars, Krav Noblis was willing, through the hunter's stewardship of ''The Adjudicator'', to pass on knowledge to others in the same field. Noblis was concerned with the effect the Mandalorian invasion would have on the bounty hunting market, considering them "spoilsports" only on par with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Noblis willingly admitted to being in the field for the money<ref name="outerrim" /> and held a deep distaste for [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] droids—although Noblis considered it a smart move for bounty hunters to use droids as backup, Noblis despised autonomous droids that the hunter felt were out to take Noblis' livelihood. Noblis frequently penned columns for ''The Adjudicator'' and wrote with a very informal, conversational style. Noblis had pointed, fuzzy [[Ear/Legends|ears]].<ref name="tools" />

==Behind the scenes==
Krav Noblis first appeared as the author of ''[[The Adjudicator Special Report: The Outer Rim]]'', an [[in-universe]] newsletter appearing on the last page of [[Knights of the Old Republic 14|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 14]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]], published by [[Dark Horse Comics]],<ref name="outerrim" /> and released on [[February 28]], [[2007]].<ref name="DH">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/14-395/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-14|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #14}}</ref>
